## Issuer Registration

Thank you for contributing to the open agent internet!

### How Registration Works

Registration is **automated and permissionless**. To ensure the integrity of the Open Trust Registry, please ensure your PR meets the following acceptance criteria:

### Automated Verification Checklist (Tier 1)

- [ ] **Valid Ed25519 Key:** I have generated my keypair using `npx @open-agent-trust/cli keygen --issuer-id <MY_ID>`.
- [ ] **Proof-of-Key-Ownership:** I have included `registry/proofs/<MY_ID>.proof` generated by the `prove` CLI command.
- [ ] **Domain Verification:** My website hosts `/.well-known/agent-trust.json` containing my `issuer_id` and public key fingerprint (`kid`).

If all three checks pass, the CI pipeline will merge this PR automatically.

### Required Files

Your PR must contain exactly two files:
- `registry/issuers/<MY_ID>.json` — your issuer entry
- `registry/proofs/<MY_ID>.proof` — your proof of key ownership

### Generating the Proof

```bash
npx @open-agent-trust/cli prove \
  --issuer-id <MY_ID> \
  --private-key <MY_ID>.private.pem
```

This produces a proof file like:
```
-----BEGIN OATR KEY OWNERSHIP PROOF-----
Canonical-Message: oatr-proof-v1:<MY_ID>
Signature: <base64url-encoded-ed25519-signature>
-----END OATR KEY OWNERSHIP PROOF-----
```

The proof is a detached Ed25519 signature over the canonical message. Your private key is used for signing but **never appears** in the proof file. See [spec 11 — Proof of Key Ownership](spec/11-proof-of-key-ownership.md) for the full format specification.

### Domain Verification

Host a JSON file at `https://yourdomain.com/.well-known/agent-trust.json`:
```json
{
  "issuer_id": "<MY_ID>",
  "public_key_fingerprint": "<KID_FROM_KEYGEN>"
}
```

The `public_key_fingerprint` is the `kid` printed during `keygen` (format: `{issuer-id}-{YYYY-MM}`).

### Capability Verification (Tier 2 — Optional)

Your `capabilities` block (supervision model, audit logging, etc.) will initially be marked as `"capabilities_verified": false`. Community auditors may review and verify your claims in a separate PR.

**Your inclusion in the registry does not depend on this step.**

### What Happens Next

Once merged, your issuer entry will be included in the next signed `manifest.json` during the scheduled signing ceremony. See `GOVERNANCE.md` for details on the governance model.
